  "findings": [
    "Aluminum conductor terminated on a device not rated for CO/ALR connection",
    "High risk of galvanic corrosion, loose connections, and potential fire hazard"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace the non-compliant device with one rated for CO/ALR",
    "Alternatively, pig-tail the aluminum conductor to a copper conductor using an approved splicing connector (e.g., Al/Cu rated wire nut) before terminating on the device"
  ],
  "rationale": "Terminating aluminum conductors on devices not specifically rated and marked CO/ALR (or AL/CU for larger equipment) violates NEC requirements. Aluminum and copper have different thermal expansion rates and electrochemical properties, which can lead to loose connections, overheating, and electrical fires if incompatible terminals are used.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14",
    "NEC 406.3",
    "NEC 310"
  ]
}
